subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Region R: y=sin(πx) and y=x³-4x — Area, Horizontal Split (Write Only), Square Cross Sections & Pond Volume
stimulus: Let $R$ be the region bounded by the graphs of $y = \sin(\pi x)$ and $y = x^3 - 4x$, as shown in the figure above.

rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct limits: $\sin(\pi x) = x^3 - 4x$ at $x = 0$ and $x = 2$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Area $= \int_0^2 (\sin(\pi x) - (x^3-4x))\, dx = 4$.

parts.prompt: The horizontal line $y = -2$ splits the region $R$ into two parts. Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression for the area of the part of $R$ that is below this horizontal line.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $x^3-4x = -2$ at $r \approx 0.5392$ and $s \approx 1.6751$. Area below $y=-2$ is $\int_r^s (-2-(x^3-4x))\, dx$ (do not evaluate).

parts.prompt: The region $R$ is the base of a solid. For this solid, each cross section perpendicular to the $x$-axis is a square. Find the volume of this solid.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Volume $= \int_0^2 (\sin(\pi x)-(x^3-4x))^2\, dx \approx 9.978$.

parts.prompt: The region $R$ models the surface of a small pond. At all points in $R$ at a distance $x$ from the $y$-axis, the depth of the water is given by $h(x) = 3 - x$. Find the volume of water in the pond.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Volume $= \int_0^2 (3-x)(\sin(\pi x)-(x^3-4x))\, dx \approx 8.369$ or $8.370$.
